working_on: Diagnose the post-S-0186 MemPalace substrate regression and land the originally-planned ADR 0079 rebuild-tool amendment.
outcome_summary: Diagnostic surfaced 3 coupled root causes for the post-S-0186 regression: (C1) rebuild-tool inherits _HNSW_BLOAT_GUARD sync_threshold=50_000 at create-collection time → chromadb _persist() never fires during rebuild adds; (C2) S-0186 defensive prune classifier hardcoded em_t.key='created_at' but mempalace writes 'filed_at' → post_prune_deleted=0 every fire; (C3) S-0186 psycopg connect-timeout fix is inert against Supabase pgbouncer transaction-pool URL (silently strips options=-c). Five inline fixes landed across 5 commits: validate.py client-side wall-clock query watchdog; mempalace_post_hook_prune.py field-name correction; mempalace_rebuild_hnsw.py ADR 0079 amendment (sync_threshold=3 at create + raise to 100 post-adds); subsidiary embed-fn check fix for chromadb 1.5.x; ADR 0079 amendment + findings report. 7 new tests. Empirical verification: rebuild produced verified-non-zero state on scratch palace (link_lists.bin=6044+272 bytes); audit-mode prune drained 745→678 drawers cleanly. NEW critical finding (Issue #134): atomic-swap into live destroyed rebuilt state within seconds of first MCP invocation (link_lists.bin → 0 bytes; index_metadata.pickle deleted). 4 GitHub Issues filed: #134 (MCP-open destroys rebuild — CRITICAL), #135 (pgbouncer-strips-options), #136 (hook-log fragmentation across 26 worktrees), #137 (mempalace_closets threshold not migrated). 11th consecutive ADR 0053 negative finding.
scope_delivery_notes: Originally-planned ADR 0079 rebuild-tool amendment landed correctly and verified non-zero on scratch palace. Empirical re-verification of Mode 2 search post-swap did NOT close: atomic-swap into live triggered destruction of rebuilt state by MCP-server-open path (within seconds). This is a NEW failure layer (Issue #134) not anticipated by the original plan or by ADR 0090. The amendment is correct at the rebuild boundary; the swap boundary needs separate work. Scope expanded inline (with-user-confirmation) to include the validate.py watchdog fix and the field-name bug fix, both of which were preconditions or coupled root causes the diagnostic surfaced. 5 commits landed; 4 Issues filed; substantive scope delivered.
next_session_handle: Issue #134 (MCP-server-open destroys rebuilt segments after swap) is the critical follow-up. Without that fix, the in-rebuild threshold override is correct but the substrate stays broken at the swap boundary. Investigation needed: lsof capture during swap; test whether kill-MCP-first preserves rebuild state; update mempalace-operations.md swap procedure.
